subtitle: A string of hidden coves under the brilliant white chalk cliffs known as the White Rocks, above dark, scorching sand and clear deep water on Cyprus's south coast — a beach that carries the island's British past in its very name and its nearest airfield.
spike_statement: Hidden coves under brilliant white 'White Rocks' chalk cliffs above dark, scorching sand — a Cyprus beach named for a British governor, near Britain's last island base.

levers.photo_tone: brilliant white chalk cliffs above dark grey-black sand and deep clear turquoise water, small hidden coves cut into the shore, harsh bright Cypriot summer light, a little wooden jetty
